description:
You are a data-scientist who has come back to an old project that lives in the home
directory.  
A small “sales” dataset is stored in

    /home/user/datasets/raw_sales.csv

Your job is to do a very small data-cleaning pass on that file and create two new
artefacts plus an audit log.  Work only with standard command-line tools that are
commonly available in a fresh Debian/Ubuntu installation (e.g. awk, grep, sed,
cut, sort, uniq, wc, head, tail, date, printf, etc.).  You do NOT need any
Python/R/conda environments.

FINAL REQUIRED FILES
────────────────────

1.  Cleaned data set  
    Path: /home/user/datasets/cleaned_sales.csv  
    Format: a **CSV** file that must contain **exactly** the header line

        order_id,order_date,region,sales

    followed by the records that meet all of the following rules:

        • Every field is non-empty.  
        • order_date is normalised to the ISO form YYYY-MM-DD
          (zero-padded months and days, four-digit year, hyphen separator).  
        • Fields retain the same column order as the header.  
        • No surrounding quotation marks are allowed around any field.  
        • Lines end with a single UNIX newline “\n”; no extra blank lines.

2.  Summary statistics  
    Path: /home/user/datasets/summary_stats.json  
    Format: **one single-line JSON object** with the three keys in the exact
    listed order, and no spaces:

        {"count_orders":<INT>,"total_sales":<FLOAT>,"average_sales":<FLOAT>}

    • count_orders  – integer number of rows that appear in
      cleaned_sales.csv (excluding its header).  
    • total_sales   – sum of the numeric sales column for those clean rows,
      shown with exactly two digits after the decimal point.  
    • average_sales – arithmetic mean of the sales values for those clean rows,
      also with exactly two digits after the decimal point.  
    The automated checker will expect the keys in that precise order, the
    colon “:” as the delimiter, numbers without quotation marks, and the entire
    object on one logical line.

3.  Cleaning activity log  
    Path: /home/user/logs/cleaning.log  
    Format: **tab-separated audit trail** (ASCII <TAB>, i.e. “\t”) containing
    the six lines listed below verbatim — same wording, same capitalisation,
    same number of TAB characters (two per line).  Each line ends with a single
    UNIX newline and nothing else.

        1\tLOAD_DATA\traw_sales.csv loaded
        2\tDROP_MISSING_ROWS\t3 rows removed
        3\tSTANDARDIZE_DATES\tdates converted to YYYY-MM-DD
        4\tEXPORT_CLEAN\tcleaned_sales.csv written
        5\tCALC_STATS\tsummary statistics computed
        6\tEXPORT_STATS\tsummary_stats.json written

    The log must be created under /home/user/logs (create the directory if it
    does not exist).  Make sure the permissions allow the automated tester to
    read the file (the default “umask 0022” is fine).

VALIDATION RULES (what the grader will look for)
───────────────────────────────────────────────
• /home/user/datasets/cleaned_sales.csv exists, has the correct header, the
  correct remaining rows, and nothing else.  
• /home/user/datasets/summary_stats.json exists and contains exactly one JSON
  line that matches the specification (correct key order, numeric precision,
  correct values).  
• /home/user/logs/cleaning.log exists, is readable, and its six lines match the
  template shown above **character-for-character** (including TABs, not spaces).

Hints to get you started (do NOT echo the commands here):
• Inspect the raw CSV and count how many rows contain empty fields.  
• Convert any dates written as “YYYY/MM/DD” into “YYYY-MM-DD”.  
• Basic awk or sed pipelines are fully sufficient for this size of data.  

When you are finished, you can use “cat”, “head”, “tail”, “wc -l”, “jq -c .”
etc. to double-check your three deliverables, but remember that only the three
files listed above will be graded.
truth:
Initial file(s) already present before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/datasets/raw_sales.csv
   ─────────────────────────────────
   order_id,order_date,region,sales
   A001,2020-01-02,North,100.50
   A002,2020/01/05,South,200
   A003,2020-01-07,,150
   ,2020-01-10,East,250.75
   A005,2020-01-12,West,

Desired final state after successful task completion:

1. /home/user/datasets/cleaned_sales.csv
   ─────────────────────────────────────
   order_id,order_date,region,sales
   A001,2020-01-02,North,100.50
   A002,2020-01-05,South,200.00

   (Note: “200” in raw becomes “200.00” acceptable; either “200” or
    “200.00” will be accepted as the sales column has no prescribed
    decimal-place requirement in the task description, but the grader
    will permit both.  The two surviving rows are A001 and A002.)

2. /home/user/datasets/summary_stats.json
   ──────────────────────────────────────
   {"count_orders":2,"total_sales":300.50,"average_sales":150.25}

3. /home/user/logs/cleaning.log
   ────────────────────────────
   1	LOAD_DATA	raw_sales.csv loaded
   2	DROP_MISSING_ROWS	3 rows removed
   3	STANDARDIZE_DATES	dates converted to YYYY-MM-DD
   4	EXPORT_CLEAN	cleaned_sales.csv written
   5	CALC_STATS	summary statistics computed
   6	EXPORT_STATS	summary_stats.json written

All three files must be world-readable (0664 is fine) and the directories
/home/user/datasets and /home/user/logs must exist and be world-executable
(0755 is fine).
